title: The Pessimist
content:
The pessimistic locust, last to leaf,
    Though all the world is glad, still talks of grief.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Ella Wheeler Wilcox, titled "The Pessimist", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
